They are dying to tell their story. And so, like one of my great questions is, how did you get in this business anyway? Well, they're dying to tell that story. And they've polished that story, and they've worked on that story, and they got that story perfected. It's the equivalent of the, how did you two meet anyway, story. See, every couple's got that story, and they've fixed it. You know, so the real story is we were both drunk on our ass, and we met in this bar, and you know, the sex in the backseat of the car was pretty good, and we've been together ever since. Well, they've turned that into, we were in Paris, and at the Eiffel Tower, and so they got this thing down, right? And they got, and usually they got it as a little two-person George Burns and Gracie Comedy Act. So they're just dying to have this question asked, and they trot out their act, right? Well, this guy's dying to tell this story. He's dying to tell this story. And then it's like the, you know, the, and you ask essay questions, you don't ask, you know, yes or no questions. You ask essay questions, you know, so there's, you know, you know, what's the biggest change that's happened in your business or your industry in the past year, you know, what's really changing in the farm and implement business? And now you've got to really, like, be interested in what's really changing in the farm and implement business, because he's dying to tell you. And then, what drives you nuts? You know, he's dying to tell you that story and complain about usually employees and, you know, now you can direct it now. You can start to direct it into whatever it is that you sell. So my directed questions then are, how do you feel about your advertising and your marketing? By the way, I deliberately used the word feel. And now they tell you the shit doesn't work and I hate the ad agencies and they go on and on and on and on. If I wasn't selling advertising and marketing, I'd direct it somewhere else, right? So you can start to direct it where you want it to go. And you keep asking questions and listening and nodding and grunting and appropriately reacting for as long as they'll talk. 20-minute interviews turn into two, three, four hours without difficulty at all. I've made some of the biggest sales in my life this way. You just like have to be really, really, really patient. But they'll, like Bill said this morning, you ask the right questions and the client, like, you know, figures out his own answer. If you ask the right questions, they now buy you. They make the sale for themselves. If you let them talk long enough, they arrive at the point of, what can you do for me? You know, how does this work? All right? And so that's what you got to do when you sell yourself in cold. And of course, you got to wow them, all right? If you're going to send them stuff at some point, yeah, can't be sending them a damn brochure. Everybody sends them a brochure. They got fireplaces for that. You know? I mean, especially in a niche, if you see their mail in, you know, in the speaking business, meeting planners, it goes in cycles. When I was chasing speaking gigs a lot, it was the trifold brochure. So everybody had a trifold brochure, right? With a picture on the front of them with a microphone and no audience. And, you know, our old joke used to be a picture on the front with a microphone, no audience inside a picture of them standing next to Norman Fitzipield with Norman trying to get away from them like he's trying to catch a bus, you know? And that was everybody's trifold brochure. So the meeting planner, they've got them laid out on a table, they all look alike. You know, they're interchangeable, right? And this goes on everywhere. Today in our business, the speaking business, by the way, it's the one sheet. So everybody's figured out they can get cheap. You know, hell, trifold brochure. Everybody wants a one sheet so we can email it. Jesus. But everybody does it. So if you're going to send them something, you better wow them. Like, you better send them a lot of stuff. And you better send them like all the AMO you got.
